{"key_event":"1973 military coup and authoritarian repression","reasoning":"Pre-1973 mobilization briefly pushed civil society and state intervention higher, but by 1977 parties, unions, Congress, media, universities, and popular organizations had been banned, purged, or driven underground. The junta’s coercive and technocratic executive apparatus gave the state overwhelming enforcement capacity despite reduced developmental-economic functions under neoliberal reform.","society_change":-5.1,"society_power":3.2,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":9.2}
